factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all segments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased due to a $1.37 billion increase in revenues from our Renewable Energy segment, a $620.4 million increase in revenues from our Electric Power segment and a $454.9 million increase in revenues from our Underground and Infrastructure segment.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows significant revenue increases across multiple segments, indicating strong demand growth.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher demand segments.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues was primarily due to increased demand and improved supply chain dynamics associated with components for renewable generation projects, as well as approximately $65 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shift towards higher demand segments suggests improved revenue quality, though not yet a major breakthrough.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2023 was primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers and approximately $55 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence of growing unit volumes and transaction counts supports moderate growth.

factors.summary: All end markets are growing, with the Renewable Energy segment leading the growth.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 was primarily due to increased demand and improved supply chain dynamics associated with certain components for renewable generation projects, as well as approximately $175 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: All end markets growing, with Renewable Energy leading, indicates strong positive momentum.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure.

evidence.text: The increase in corporate and non-allocated costs during the three months ended September 30, 2023 included a $4.2 million increase in amortization expense and an aggregate increase of $17.1 million largely comprised of an increase in compensation expense, primarily related to non-cash stock compensation expense and salaries in support of business growth; consulting fees; and change in fair value of contingent consideration liabilities.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs contribute to margin pressure, indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are stable or improving slightly.

evidence.text: Operating margin during the three months ended September 30, 2023 was negatively impacted by normal variability in overall project timing and increased unabsorbed costs related to higher levels of fixed costs for resources required to support a portion of the increase in project activity experienced in the third quarter of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Margins are stable or improving slightly, with mixed signals from project timing and costs.

factors.summary: Management appears confident in business growth.

evidence.text: The increase in corporate and non-allocated costs during the three months ended September 30, 2023 included a $4.2 million increase in amortization expense and an aggregate increase of $17.1 million largely comprised of an increase in compensation expense, primarily related to non-cash stock compensation expense and salaries in support of business growth; consulting fees; and change in fair value of contingent consideration liabilities.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Evidence suggests management is supporting growth but also facing cost pressures, indicating mixed signals.

factors.summary: Interest rates and foreign exchange are key sensitivities.

evidence.text: The tax rate for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 was favorably impacted by the recognition of a $34.0 million benefit that resulted from equity incentive awards vesting at a higher fair market value than their grant date fair market value, as compared to the recognition of $22.7 million associated with this tax benefit for the nine months ended September 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Interest rates and foreign exchange are noted sensitivities without clear positive or negative implications.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are creating growing demand.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 was primarily due to increased spending by our utility customers and approximately $200 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Electric Power Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased spending by utility customers and acquired businesses indicate growth.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind with strong demand.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2023 was primarily due to increased demand and improved supply chain dynamics associated with certain components for renewable generation projects, as well as approximately $175 million in revenues attributable to acquired businesses.
evidence.subsection: Renewable Energy Infrastructure Solutions Segment Results

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong demand and improved supply chain dynamics are tailwinds.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are increasing financing expenses.

evidence.text: The increase in interest and other financing expenses was primarily from the impact of higher interest rates on our outstanding variable rate debt during the nine months ended September 30, 2023 as compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher interest rates are causing increased financing expenses.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are affecting revenues.

evidence.text: The net other expense for the nine months ended September 30, 2022 includes a loss of $76.5 million that resulted from the remeasurement of the fair value of our investment in Starry.
evidence.subsection: Nine months ended September 30, 2023 compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2022

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements resulted in significant financial losses.
